subject: Public Health
title: Trachoma and Facial Cleanliness
description: A fast breakdown of Trachoma and Facial Cleanliness for builders.

hook.text: Trachoma is the world's leading infectious cause of blindness—and a bar of soap could prevent most cases.

buildup.text: Chlamydia trachomatis infects the eyes—repeated infections cause scarring that turns eyelids inward, blinding slowly.

discovery.text: Flies spread the bacterium between children's eyes—especially where water for face-washing is scarce.

twist.text: The SAFE strategy—Surgery, Antibiotics, Facial cleanliness, and Environmental improvement—is eliminating trachoma.

climax.text: 15 countries have eliminated trachoma since 2012—proving that WASH-focused approaches work.

punchline.text: Clean faces prevent blindness. Water access makes it possible.

quiz.question: What does the 'F' in the SAFE strategy for trachoma stand for?
